fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the clamping block should execute; the condition `not clamp` means it executes when clamp is False instead — this expr is False on failing tests where clamp=True but clamping is skipped.
Inside the then-branch of `if not clamp`, clamp must be False; if clamp is True we should never be here.
In the else-branch (no clamping applied), clamp must be True — but the inverted condition means clamp=True lands here without clamping.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the final y value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the bug causes this to be violated.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamping is requested the returned value must be within the supply range of the two endpoints.
